title: Content Designer
description: Designs content-first experiences, combining writing, information architecture, and user research to solve problems through language and structure.

riskFactors: Develop Web site maps, application models, image templates, or page templates that meet project goals, user needs, or industry standards.
Develop, validate, and document test routines and schedules to ensure that test cases mimic external interfaces and address all browser and device types.
Write and edit technical documentation for digital interface products and designs, such as user manuals, testing protocols, and reports.
protectiveFactors: Collaborate with management or users to develop e-commerce strategies and to integrate these strategies with Web sites.
Collaborate with web development professionals, such as front-end or back-end developers, to complete the full scope of Web development projects.
Confer with management or development teams to prioritize needs, resolve conflicts, develop content criteria, or choose solutions.
aiImpact.scoreExplainer: Based on analysis of 30 O*NET task descriptions (40% weight) combined with tool displacement index (60% weight).
